safetyNotes: EMA: GI reactions (nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea) and allergic reactions (urticaria, skin rash, dyspnoea, anaphylactic reaction) have been reported (frequency not known). Pregnancy/lactation safety not established; use not recommended absent sufficient data.

sources.title: EU herbal monograph on *Hedera helix* L., folium (EMA)
*Hedera helix* L. - Plants of the World Online (Kew)
EMA monograph excerpt (interactions/safety section screenshot source)

description: Ivy leaf preparations are defined as specific dry or liquid extracts with stated drug-extract ratios (DER) and extraction solvents (ethanol at various concentrations; propylene-glycol mixture for soft extract).
dosage: EMA dosing depends on preparation type; examples shown for adolescents/adults/elderly include: - Preparation a): single 15–65 mg, 1–3× daily; daily 45–105 mg (note special maximum for ethanol-containing products) - Preparation b): single 14–18 mg, 3× daily; daily 42–54 mg - Preparation c): single 33 mg, 2× daily; daily 66 mg - Preparation d): single 100 mg, 3× daily; daily 300 mg - Preparation e): single 40 mg, 3× daily; daily 120 mg Pediatric dosing ranges are also specified; use under age 2 is contraindicated.
interactions: EMA reports "None reported" for interactions with other medicinal products and other forms of interaction (in the monograph section shown).
legalStatus: Legal; used in registered cough preparations
preparation: EMA-defined extract preparations (dry/liquid/soft extracts with specified DER and solvents), administered orally; duration guidance: consult clinician if symptoms persist >1 week.
region: POWO lists native range as Europe/Mediterranean region to Iran.
summary: EMA monograph supports use of *Hedera helix* leaf preparations as an expectorant in productive cough, with defined extract preparations and dosing schedules. EMA reports "none reported" for interactions in the monograph section shown, and states pregnancy/lactation safety not established (use not recommended).
